user_system_path: Identity-architecture seam (#511), Stage 1: a link-guard so one external account maps to one Foldera identity. In both OAuth connect callbacks (app/api/google/callback, app/api/microsoft/callback), before persisting the token, reject linking a (provider, email) already linked and not-disconnected under a DIFFERENT user_id, redirecting with a clear already_linked_elsewhere message instead of silently stealing the refresh token. New helper lib/auth/user-tokens.ts:findCrossUserTokenConflict (fails open on query error; self-reconnect allowed; case-insensitive email). Friendly copy in app/dashboard/settings/SettingsClient.tsx. This is the durable fix for the recurring Google reconnect and the split-brain accounts (#509). Stage 2-4 (Google sole sign-in, Microsoft demoted to a linkable surface, migrate the Microsoft-identity owner account, remove AzureADProvider) are owner-gated follow-ups in #511.
allowed_files_raw: Stage 1 link-guard: lib/auth/user-tokens.ts (new findCrossUserTokenConflict) + its test; app/api/google/callback/route.ts and app/api/microsoft/callback/route.ts (call the guard before saveUserToken) + their tests; app/dashboard/settings/SettingsClient.tsx (already_linked_elsewhere copy). Control-plane governance files. NO NextAuth provider removal and NO user-data migration in this PR (that is Stage 3, owner-gated).
forbidden_files_raw: No Stripe, Scout, schema/migration, or secrets changes. No removal of AzureADProvider and no auth-identity migration in Stage 1 (owner-gated, separate). No auto-send.
required_local_proof: npm run gate:continuity && npm run typecheck && npx vitest run lib/auth/__tests__/user-tokens.test.ts app/api/google/callback/__tests__/route.test.ts app/api/microsoft/callback/__tests__/route.test.ts
required_browser_proof: Live: a user connecting a Google/Microsoft account already linked to another Foldera login is blocked with the already_linked_elsewhere message (no token write), while reconnecting their OWN account still succeeds. Owner-side validation on the Sources/settings screen.
browser_proof_command: manual: in the dashboard settings, attempt to connect an external account already linked to a different Foldera user and confirm the already_linked_elsewhere message; then reconnect the owner's own account and confirm success.
acceptance_condition: Both OAuth connect callbacks call findCrossUserTokenConflict before saveUserToken and refuse a cross-user link with ?{provider}_error=already_linked_elsewhere; self-reconnect is unaffected; the guard fails open on a query error. gate:continuity + typecheck green; the three named test suites green. No NextAuth provider change and no data migration in this PR.
anti_regression_checks: google + microsoft callback tests green (incl. the new cross-user refusal cases)
saveUserToken still called for same-user reconnect (no false positives)
no edits under app/api/stripe, lib/scout, supabase/migrations; AzureADProvider untouched
stop_condition: Stage 1 link-guard lands as a PR on claude/identity-link-guard-511 targeting #511; gate:continuity + typecheck + the three test suites green. Stage 2-4 (identity switch + migration) remain owner-gated in #511. Live cross-account refusal is owner-validated (BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_RECEIPT until then).
next_command: Owner: merge the Stage 1 guard. Then decide on Stage 2-4 of #511 (Google sole sign-in + Microsoft as a linked surface + migrate the Microsoft-identity owner account). Also still pending: re-consent Google on the owner account (#509) and Scout #494 activation.
